invariantResults.evidence: All inline code references in writer-added content are properly backticked. Lines 31: `ApplicationRole`, `ApplicationRoleStore`, `AddIdentity`, `AddRoleStore` — all correct.

Code examples are broken into logical sections (Password policy, Password reset, Email confirmation), each with its own code block and contextual prose. Writer maintained this structure when updating type parameters.
Writer-added content (info callout) does not contain step-by-step instructions. No ordered/unordered list misuse introduced.
No headings were added or modified by the writer. All existing headings use sentence case correctly.
Writer-added callout uses active voice throughout: 'The Identity configuration...must register', 'These services enable', 'See the code samples'.
Writer-added content uses present simple tense: 'must register', 'enable', 'See'.
No British English spellings in writer-added content.
Writer uses consistent terminology: 'member role' (matching REC-013), 'ApplicationRole'/'ApplicationRoleStore' throughout all modified code samples.
Writer-added callout avoids the pronoun 'it'. Uses 'These services' with clear antecedent.
No use of 'please' in writer-added content.
Callout is concise and direct — three sentences covering the requirement, its purpose, and where to find examples.
Callout links to member-roles page ({% page_link member_roles_xp %}) providing context for readers who land on this page first.
Line 31: 'The Identity configuration in your application must register' — 'must' is used for the configuration (not addressing the user directly), which is acceptable per INV-style-039.
No jargon or complex expressions in writer-added content.
Info callout used correctly for additional context about member role service registration requirements.
Callout has bold title '**Member role services**' on a separate line (line 29).
Callout title is 'Member role services', not 'Info' or 'Information'.
Info callout severity matches content — additional context about service registration, not a warning or critical note.
page_link identifier 'member_roles_xp' verified in member-roles.md front matter. Not hallucinated.
Link text 'member role' is descriptive and contextual, not vague.
Writer-added callout uses correct Liquid admonition syntax: {% info %} / {% endinfo %}.
All modified code blocks use {% code %} Liquid tag with lang=csharp (lines 120, 139, 197). Valid lang value.
Added 'member_roles_xp' to related_pages — member-roles.md is in the same _documentation collection.
No UI element names were added by the writer. Pre-existing bold formatting of 'Program.cs' as code title is appropriate.
Writer does not pluralize code elements. Uses proper noun phrases: '`AddIdentity` and `AddRoleStore` calls'.
Writer-added callout links to member-roles page with context, maintaining page independence. The note explains the requirement without assuming prior reading.

invariantResults.note: Writer did not introduce the ellipsis patterns. Line 143 `// ...` (pre-existing) and line 201 bare `...` (pre-existing) show inconsistency. See additionalFindings.

additionalFindings.evidence: Headings follow h2 → h3 progression correctly: ## Prerequisites, ## Implement forms authentication → ### Registration, ### Sign in and sign out, ### Password policy, ### Password reset, ## Email confirmation → ### Implement email confirmation. No skipped levels.
Front matter contains required fields: title, persona, identifier, order, license, redirect_from, toc, related_pages. Writer correctly added 'member_roles_xp' to related_pages array.
All Liquid tags in writer-added content are syntactically valid: {% info %}, {% endinfo %}, {% page_link member_roles_xp linkText="member role" %}.

Pre-existing inconsistency in modified code blocks: Line 143 uses `// ...` (ellipsis inside comment), while line 201 uses bare `...`. Per INV-codesamples-006, bare ellipsis is correct. The writer did not introduce this inconsistency but modified the surrounding code block. Consider harmonizing to bare `...` on line 143.
additionalFindings.suggestion: In the password reset code sample (line 143), change `     // ...` to `    ...` to match the pattern on line 201 and comply with INV-codesamples-006.

additionalFindings.evidence: Pre-existing indentation inconsistency in modified code blocks: Line 143 uses 5-space indent (should be 4 for C#). Line 201 uses a tab character while line 202 uses 4 spaces. Writer did not introduce these but they exist in modified sections.
additionalFindings.suggestion: Normalize indentation in both code blocks to 4 spaces for consistency.

additionalFindings.evidence: Line 17 (pre-existing, not modified by writer): 'on a login page' — per REC-013, should use 'sign-in page' instead of 'login page'. Outside task scope but worth noting for future cleanup.
additionalFindings.suggestion: Change 'login page' to 'sign-in page' on line 17 to align with REC-013 terminology.

additionalFindings.evidence: Lines 176 and 222 (pre-existing, not modified by writer): use 'click a link' and 'click the confirmation link'. INV-style-033 prohibits 'click' in favor of 'select'. However, 'click a link' is specifically about hyperlinks in email messages — 'select' may not be the natural replacement in this context. Outside task scope.
additionalFindings.suggestion: Consider rephrasing to 'follow the link' or 'open the link' on lines 176 and 222 to avoid 'click' while remaining natural.

additionalFindings.evidence: Page lacks a 'Next Steps' section at the end. INV-structure-001 requires Title, Introduction, Body, Result, and Next Steps. INV-structure-010 recommends next steps linking to related articles. Pre-existing structural gap — not in task scope.
additionalFindings.suggestion: Consider adding a '## Next steps' section at the end of the page linking to related authentication topics.

additionalFindings.evidence: Line 137 (pre-existing, not modified by writer): 'implemetnation' — typo for 'implementation'. Outside task scope but adjacent to modified code block.
additionalFindings.suggestion: Fix typo: change 'implemetnation' to 'implementation' on line 137.

issues.description: Pre-existing INV-codesamples-006 inconsistency: `// ...` uses ellipsis inside a comment. Line 201 correctly uses bare `...`. The writer modified this code block but did not introduce the issue.
Pre-existing indentation inconsistency in modified code blocks: 5 spaces (line 143), tab character (line 201) — should be 4 spaces.
issues.suggestion: Normalize to 4-space indentation in both code blocks.

issues.description: Pre-existing: 'login page' should be 'sign-in page' per REC-013 terminology. Not in task scope.
Pre-existing typo: 'implemetnation' should be 'implementation'. Not in task scope but adjacent to modified content.
Pre-existing: Page lacks a 'Next Steps' section required by INV-structure-001. Not in task scope.
issues.suggestion: Add a '## Next steps' section linking to related authentication topics.

summary.briefSummary: All task-inlined invariants (INV-formatting-004, INV-codesamples-010, INV-structure-006) and all applicable style/structure/formatting/jekyll/codesamples/crossref invariants PASS on the writer's modifications and additions. The writer-added info callout is well-structured with proper Liquid syntax, correct cross-reference identifier, descriptive link text, appropriate callout type, and compliant formatting. All four code sample updates are consistent (NoOpApplicationRole → ApplicationRole, NoOpApplicationRoleStore → ApplicationRoleStore<ApplicationRole>). Five pre-existing issues noted for future cleanup (ellipsis in comment, indentation inconsistency, 'login' terminology, typo, missing Next Steps) — none introduced by the writer and all outside the task scope.

metaKnowledge.note: Knowledge brief is cold start with no prior pipeline runs. No style evolutions or anti-patterns to apply. Anti-patterns.md not found — skipped per missing artifact handling.
